commit.message:
feat: 30 language-specific + platform skills (Rust, TS, Python, cloud, messaging, auth, AI)

Rust (5):
- rust-axum-api.md — Axum REST API with middleware, handlers, error handling
- rust-tokio-async.md — Tokio tasks, channels, streams, cancellation patterns
- rust-serde-serialization.md — JSON/TOML/YAML with serde derive attributes
- rust-error-handling.md — thiserror for libraries, anyhow for applications
- rust-testing-patterns.md — rstest, mockall, insta snapshots, llvm-cov

TypeScript (5):
- ts-nextjs-app.md — Next.js 15 App Router, Server Components, server actions
- ts-react-components.md — hooks, context, Suspense, performance patterns
- ts-prisma-database.md — Prisma ORM schema, migrations, query patterns
- ts-zod-validation.md — Zod schemas, safeParse, transforms, discriminated unions
- ts-express-api.md — Express router, async error handling, Supertest

Python (5):
- python-fastapi.md — FastAPI routers, dependency injection, lifespan
- python-pandas-data.md — DataFrame load/clean/transform/aggregate/export
- python-pytest.md — fixtures, parametrize, mock, async tests, coverage
- python-pydantic.md — Pydantic v2 models, validators, settings management
- python-sqlalchemy.md — SQLAlchemy 2.x ORM, async sessions, Alembic

Cloud/Platform (15):
- cloud-aws-deployment.md — Lambda, ECS, S3, RDS, IAM, OIDC CI/CD
- cloud-gcp-deployment.md — Cloud Run, GCS, Cloud SQL, Workload Identity
- cloud-azure-deployment.md — Container Apps, Functions, Blob, Key Vault
- messaging-kafka.md — producers, consumers, partitions, schema registry
- messaging-rabbitmq.md — exchanges, routing, DLX, reliability patterns
- monitoring-prometheus.md — metrics instrumentation, Grafana queries, alerting
- monitoring-opentelemetry.md — traces, metrics, logs, OTel Collector config
- auth-oauth2.md — Authorization Code + PKCE, client credentials, token validation
- auth-jwt.md — access/refresh tokens, rotation, bcrypt password hashing
- integration-graphql.md — schema-first SDL, DataLoader, Relay pagination
- integration-grpc.md — proto design, code gen, streaming, deadlines
- integration-webhook.md — HMAC verification, idempotency, retry with backoff
- data-redis-caching.md — cache-aside, distributed locks, rate limiting, pub/sub
- data-elasticsearch.md — mapping design, full-text search, aggregations, reindex
- ai-langchain.md — LCEL chains, RAG pipelines, tool-using agents, streaming

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): point release-please to crate path, not workspace root

release-please always fetches root Cargo.toml and tries to parse it
as a Rust package manifest. By setting the package path to
'crates/convergio-types' instead of '.', it only looks at the crate
Cargo.toml which has [package].

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Use custom PAM module name to avoid conflict with hyprlock package

Rename the managed PAM service from 'hyprlock' to 'hyprlock-custom' so
the dotfiles-managed /etc/pam.d/hyprlock-custom does not conflict with
the package-owned /etc/pam.d/hyprlock. Point hyprlock.conf at the
custom module via auth { pam:module = hyprlock-custom }.

commit.message:
Add progress feedback for large collection fetching (#21)

Closes #20 
Large collections (hundreds of items) produce no output while resolving
external IDs per-item via HTTP, making the tool appear hung.

### Changes

- **`plex.Client.OnProgress` callback** — called with `(current, total)`
after each item is processed in `GetCollectionItems`, where the per-item
metadata lookups are the bottleneck
- **High-level status in `resolveCollection`** — prints
finding/fetching/loaded messages to stdout so the user sees activity
immediately
- **Stderr progress line in `main()`** — `newCollectionProgressFunc`
writes `\rProcessing collection items: N/M` with in-place
carriage-return updates; newline on completion

```
Finding collection "Movies"...
Fetching items for "Movies"...
Processing collection items: 142/200   ← stderr, in-place
Loaded 200 items from "Movies"
```

The callback is nil-safe — no behavior change when unset (e.g., in tests
or the bridge package).

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: nipuna-perera <11575062+nipuna-perera@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Nipuna perera <nipuna.royal@gmail.com>

Publish v2: add mariadb importer (#11)

* feat: add SecForm4MariadbInserter node and MySqlCredentials

Add a new n8n node that takes parsed SEC Form 4 data and upserts it
into a MySQL/MariaDB database. Features:

- MySqlCredentials credential type for database connection
- Idempotent upserts using INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE
- Automatic table creation (10 normalized tables) via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS
- Transactional writes with rollback on error
- Configurable table prefix (default: sec_)
- 18-test suite covering SQL generation, transactions, and rollback

commit.message:
Replace getUserMedia with WebXR Raw Camera Access (camera-access)

Android Chrome では WebXR AR セッション中に getUserMedia が
カメラにアクセスできない（NotReadableError）ため、
S23 などで撮影時の背景が黒になる問題を根本修正。

【変更内容】

index.html:
  - webxr optionalFeatures に camera-access を追加

js/modes.js:
  - getUserMedia / cameraStream / cameraVideo を完全削除
  - enterShootingMode / exitShootingMode からストリーム管理を除去

js/hit-test.js (photo-capture system):
  - enter-vr で XRWebGLBinding を生成して保持
  - tock() でカメラテクスチャ取得
    XRWebGLBinding.getCameraImage(view.camera) → WebGLTexture
  - ShaderMaterial の全画面クワッドで背景レンダリング (Pass 1)
  - renderer.xr.enabled=false でキャラクター合成 (Pass 2)
  - フォールバック: view.camera が null の場合はキャラのみ透明背景

js/main.js:
  - getCameraVideo インポートを削除
  - capturePhoto を arCanvas をそのまま使う形にシンプル化

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Deep testing: 37 new tests, 4 bug fixes

Bugs fixed (found by GPT-5.4 and GPT-5.3-Codex stress testing):

- MatterStore.list(limit=0) returned all entities instead of none;
  now correctly honors zero and clamps negative limits
- compute_deadline parsed YYYY-MM-DD dates in UTC, causing off-by-one
  errors on holiday/weekend boundaries; fixed with local date parsing
- generate_invoice accepted zero/negative hours without warning;
  now skips invalid entries and returns warnings array
- process_document reported lineCount=1 for empty content; fixed to 0

New test files:
- tests/agent/stress-scenarios.test.ts (29 tests): MatterStore
  isolation across 100 entities and 5 matters, unicode/special chars,
  entity update semantics, deletion + link cleanup, search relevance,
  linking chains and edge cases, tool execution errors, system prompt
  verification, conflict checker, chronology engine edge cases
- tests/agent/product-quality.test.ts (8 tests): full litigation
  lifecycle, concurrent operations, privilege marker handling, real
  FRCP deadline scenarios, document processing pipeline, invoice
  arithmetic accuracy, cross-module linking, search relevance

521 tests total, all passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Caddy checksum verification: use SHA-512 to match upstream checksums

Caddy's checksums.txt uses SHA-512 (128 hex chars), but the verification
code expected SHA-256 (64 hex chars). This caused caddyChecksumFromFile
to skip valid hash entries and fail with 'failed to find sha256 checksum'.

Changes:
- Switch from crypto/sha256 to crypto/sha512
- Update hash length check from 64 to 128
- Rename sha256File to sha512File
- Update test to verify SHA-512 behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(bot): extract BFS crawler with Fetcher abstraction

Decouple BFS traversal logic from HTTP fetching to enable unit testing.

**Changes:**
- Add crawler package with Fetcher interface
  - FileFetcher for file-based testing (no network required)
  - HTTPFetcher for production HTTP requests
- Implement complete BFS with FIFO queue
- Add URL utilities (normalize, domain validation, extension filtering)
- Add HTML link extraction with golang.org/x/net/html
- Create 17 unit tests with 84.2% coverage
- Update bot/README.md with crawler usage examples

**Architecture:**
- Dependency inversion: BFSCrawler depends on Fetcher interface
- Strategy pattern: swap FileFetcher/HTTPFetcher without changing crawler
- Context-aware: all operations support cancellation

**Testing:**
- All tests pass using FileFetcher with HTML fixtures
- No network required for testing
- Tests verify: depth limits, domain filtering, BFS order, deduplication

**OpenSpec:**
- Modified bot-pioneer-crawler spec (added Fetcher scenarios)
- Archived change: openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-12-simple-bfs-crawler
- Pioneer integration deferred (4 tasks remaining)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

analyzer: expand stdlib map and interface resolution

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add BearSSL amalgamation for Linux TLS — all platforms working

Phase 2: Linux TLS via BearSSL single-file amalgamation
- bearssl_amalg.c: 959KB amalgamation of 95 BearSSL source files (client-only)
  - i31 big-integer engine, constant-time AES, ECDHE+RSA, ChaCha20/Poly1305
  - Zero malloc — all caller-allocated. Only needs memcpy/memcmp/memmove/strlen
  - MIT license (Thomas Pornin)
- l_tls.h: Linux backend uses BearSSL via br_sslio for I/O
  - No-verify X.509 engine (no embedded CAs — production should load from filesystem)
  - Entropy seeded via new l_getrandom() syscall wrapper
- l_os.h: Added l_getrandom() for all architectures
  - Linux: getrandom(2) — x86_64(318), aarch64/riscv(278), arm32(384)
  - Windows: RtlGenRandom (SystemFunction036) via advapi32.dll
- L_TLS_AVAILABLE now 1 on both Windows AND Linux
- misc/gen_bearssl_amalg.py: script to regenerate amalgamation from BearSSL source

CI: 10/10 targets pass (Win, Lin gcc+clang, ARM32 gcc+clang, AArch64 gcc+clang)
README: Updated all references from stubs/Windows-only to full cross-platform

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Skip redundant normalized cache lookup when raw key was already checked

When can_use_raw_cache_key is true, the normalized key is identical to
the raw key that was already checked earlier. Wrap the normalized cache
lookup in `if (!can_use_raw_cache_key)` to avoid a redundant hash-map
lookup on the common path where all args are explicitly provided.

commit.message:
Fix character facing: set rotation after DOM connection via object3D

setAttribute('rotation') was called before appendChild, so A-Frame's
component initialisation timing could override or ignore the value.

Fix: move the Y-rotation calculation to after dom.charactersContainer
.appendChild(el) and write directly to el.object3D.rotation (THREE.js
radians, no A-Frame component involved).

atan2(dx, dz) rotates the entity so its local +Z (PlaneGeometry front
face) points toward the camera, making the character always face the user.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix capture: use tock() + disable xr.enabled + physical pixels

【サイズ巨大の原因】
tick() 内で renderer.render() を呼ぶと XR の ArrayCamera が
デバイス物理ピクセル座標の viewport を持ったまま介入し、
CSS ピクセルサイズの RenderTarget に対して拡大適用される。

【真っ暗の原因】
tick() は通常レンダー前に実行されるため、XR カメラ行列の
反映が不完全なフレームで描画していた。

【修正】
- tick() → tock(): 通常レンダー完了後に実行されるため
  sceneEl.camera.matrixWorld が正確な XR ポーズを保持。
- renderer.xr.enabled を一時 false: カスタム render() 呼び出しで
  XR ArrayCamera の viewport override を無効化。
- window.innerWidth/Height → renderer.domElement.width/height:
  物理ピクセル寸法を使用し AR 表示と同スケールに。
- 背景なし時は黒塗りでなく transparent に: キャラクターは見える。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
